_doc: Deterministic decision table mapping a dimension-tier profile to {confidence, match_explanation_tag}. Read by _job-matcher AFTER the v1 tier-derivation table has produced the overall tier; computed with NO LLM call. It is ADDITIVE: it never alters the tier, the tier-derivation table, or the score-cache key (still job_id:cv_hash:profile_hash:v1). rubric_version stays v1.

inputs._doc: The deriver builds a per-job profile from the scored dimensions. Each dimension's `type` is read from the rubric (user-profile.dimensions[].type, or the default-rubric tags in dimensions-default.md). A dimension with NO `type` is treated as `load-bearing`. Counts are split by type.
inputs.load_bearing_counts: object — {A, B, C, D}: how many load-bearing dimensions sit at each tier
inputs.modifying_counts: object — {A, B, C, D}: how many modifying dimensions sit at each tier
inputs.competitiveness: high | med | low | null — the Step-2 parallel signal; null for jobs scored below B. Only consulted for the `overqualified` tag, never for confidence and never for the tier.

evaluation._doc: Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom; the FIRST matching rule wins (ordered, mutually-resolved by priority). `match` predicates read the count buckets above. A predicate omitted means 'any'. This makes the mapping a pure function of the dimension-tier profile (plus competitiveness for one tag), so the same profile always yields the same {confidence, tag}.

rules.note: Every dimension (load-bearing and modifying) is A. No gaps anywhere.

Candidate is a standout (competitiveness high) and load-bearing fit is clean, but a modifying dimension is demoted (B/C/D) — they exceed the role on the core bar while a context/trajectory dimension nudges down. Distinct from trajectory-concern: here the demotion reads as the candidate being too strong for the role, not the role being wrong for them.
Core (load-bearing) fit holds — no C or D — but a modifying dimension (Domain & context or Trajectory fit) is a D. The match works on the essentials but a context/trajectory worry is flagged.
A load-bearing dimension is a D. (Overall tier is already D via the v1 table — this tag explains WHY: a core requirement is unmet.)
Two or more dimensions (load-bearing or modifying) sit at C. Any C drives confidence to low; two or more reads as multiple gaps.
Exactly one dimension at C, none at D. Any C drives confidence to low per the rule; a single C reads as one gap.
No C/D anywhere, but two or more load-bearing dimensions are only B — several soft gaps on the core bar.
Exactly one load-bearing dimension at B, the rest A, no C/D. One soft gap on the core bar.
All load-bearing dimensions are A; only a modifying dimension is B. The core fit is perfect, so confidence stays high; the single modifying B is surfaced as one-gap.

fallback._doc: Defensive default if no rule above matches (should not occur for a well-formed profile). Keeps the deriver total.

test_cases.name: two load-bearing B, rest A, no C/D
D in a modifying dimension, load-bearing clean -> trajectory-concern
competitiveness high + modifying demotion, load-bearing clean -> overqualified
D in a load-bearing dimension -> underqualified, low (overall tier already D)
all load-bearing A, one modifying B -> high, one-gap
